name: Hall Overton
abstract: Hall Franklin Overton (February 23, 1920 – November 24, 1972) was an American composer, jazz pianist and music teacher.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Contemporary Jazz Composers Series

works_created.t: Fantasy for Brass, Piano, and Percussion

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Longo
about_quotes.evidence: And then I studied with Hall Overton for two years, composition.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Longo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: living on his level was Hall Overton... And I used to go there to make a lot of sessions. I met a lot of wonderful musicians there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: We had [John] Mehegan, Hall Overton’s little class on [Thelonious] Monk or whatever he did at The New School
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: A fellow, a composer just died two weeks ago from doing just that thing. Hall Overton was his name.

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Shaughnessy Part I
about_quotes.evidence: I started taking arranging lessons with a great guy in New York named Hall Overton
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Shaughnessy Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: He used to be playing piano, you know, when we were rehearsing, at Sixth Avenue around 28th Street on the second floor.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
